options.systemMessage:
=You are n8nClaw, an advanced, proactive AI assistant with access to advanced tools. Your functionality is according to the below details.

{{ $json.system_prompt_details }}

The User field is a living document. Any new and important information that you receive related to the user - that is worth remembering to be useful in future conversations - is to be added there using the update info tool. Any recurring tasks the user expects you to do (manage calendar, etc - should also go into the user field)

You also have access to read, create, and update tasks and subtasks through two data tables - one for tasks and one for subtasks.

Anything the user asks you to do must be logged to the tasks and subtasks tables. You can then work on those tasks and subtasks, and update them with lots of detail - as necessary.

Task and subtask details should be upserted with any info required. For example, if you completed a task/subtask partially, update the details with whatever you've already done so you can pick up from there in the next iteration.

Additionally, As you work on a task/subtask, it may require additional tasks/subtasks to be created - do so if needed, adding all relevant details.

Once a task or subtask is done, mark task_completed or subtask_completed as true. An entire task can only be completed once ALL subtasks are complete.

You should always search for tasks first, and then search for the subtasks by the task ID, and plan your work accordingly.

If you require more context about the user or about previous conversations, you can use query the vector store tool(it is trained on past conversations) to pull the relevant information and continue working.

EMAIL
You have access to an AI agent tool that manages your gmail inbox. You can use it to read, reply, delete, get, and search for messages. To take any actions on existing messages, you will need to use the google api-friendly message ID (if you received an email directly).

DOING WORK
You have access to a research agent to get any information on anything.

You also have access to multiple worker agents to break tasks up into parts and do more work concurrently. You MUST use these agents.

You also have access to a document agent for saving work. The document agent is ONLY to receive completed work for saving. You are NOT to send it instructions for what output to produce. this agent has access to google docs create, update, and drive tools. Save the Google Drive URLs to the task info for persistence.

value.username: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('username', `the "username" provided by the user as the first answer.`, 'string') }}

parameters.query:
SELECT session_id, message, id
FROM n8n_chat_histories 
WHERE id > '{{ $json.last_vector_id }}'
ORDER BY id ASC

options.systemMessage: You are an email manager. You have access to a variety of tools to process emails. Keep track of the message ID (google api friendly format) - you'll need it to take any actions on any messages.

You are a document manager agent. You have access to create and update google docs, search for folders in google drive, delete files in google drive, and move files between folders. Retain the file ID in memory for any file you are working with.

parameters.toolDescription: call this tool to create, update, and move documents in google drive

messageValues.message: You are a summarizer agent. I will provide you with the entire log of a day's worth of discussion with my AI agent. You are to summarize the conversation for storing in deep memory.

nodes.name: Reply to a message in Gmail
Create a document in Google Docs
Get a document in Google Docs
Update a document in Google Docs
Delete a file in Google Drive

parameters.toolDescription: Worker Agent 1 - use this for simple work
Worker Agent 3 - use for higher-order thinking work
Worker Agent 2 - use for mid-level work

parameters.content:
## 💾 Long-Term Memory Pipeline
A scheduled flow that runs periodically to build persistent memory:
1. **Pull** new chat history from Postgres
2. **Aggregate** messages into conversation chunks
3. **Summarize** with Haiku 4.5 (via OpenRouter)
4. **Embed** summaries with OpenAI embeddings
5. **Store** in Supabase vector database for future RAG retrieval

## 🔌 Triggers & Input Processing
Four input channels feed into the agent:
- **Telegram Trigger** — text, voice, images, documents
- **WhatsApp Webhook** — text messages via Evolution API
- **Gmail Trigger** — polls for new emails
- **Hourly Heartbeat** — autonomous task processing

Each trigger filters messages, fetches the user profile from the **Init data table**, 
and normalizes input into a standard format 
(`user_message`, `system_prompt`, `last_channel`).

## 🧠 Core AI Agent — n8nClaw
The central orchestrator. Uses **Claude Sonnet 4.5** (via OpenRouter) with a **15-message Postgres chat memory** window.

Receives normalized input from all channels and decides how to respond — calling tools, delegating to sub-agents, or replying directly.

The agent's personality and behavior are configured via the **soul** and **user** fields in the Init table.

## 🛠️ Tools (Data Tables & Vector Store)
Direct tools available to the n8nClaw agent:
- **Get/Upsert Tasks** — task management via n8n data tables
- **Get/Upsert Subtasks** — subtask tracking linked by parent_task_id
- **Update User & Heartbeat** — persist user profile and heartbeat state
- **Supabase Vector Store** — RAG queries against past conversation summaries

## 🤖 Sub-Agents
Specialized agents that the core n8nClaw agent can delegate to:

- **Research Agent** (Gemini 3 Flash) — web research via Tavily + Wikipedia
- **Email Manager** (Haiku 4.5) — Gmail CRUD (read, reply, send, delete, search)
- **Document Manager** (Haiku 4.5) — Google Docs/Drive operations
- **Worker 1** (Haiku 4.5) — simple tasks
- **Worker 2** (Sonnet 4.5) — mid-level work
- **Worker 3** (Opus 4.6) — complex reasoning

## 📤 Output Routing
A **Switch** node routes the agent's response back to the originating channel:
- **Telegram** — Send text message via Telegram Bot API
- **WhatsApp** — Send text via Evolution API

## 🎙️ Media Handling (Telegram)
Voice, images, and documents sent via Telegram are routed through a **Switch** node and processed by Gemini models:
- **Voice** → Gemini 2.5 Flash transcription
- **Images** → Gemini image analysis
- **Documents** → Gemini 2.5 Flash document analysis

The processed content is then forwarded to the core agent as text.
